phase: Phase 35: Offline UI Accessibility & Evidence-Integrity Deepening
task: Task 3 (gap A2) - per-section SHA-256 content digest + in-browser tamper-evident verifier

design.scope: sha256 over canonical(JSON) of every top-level section except contract_manifest; root_digest = sha256 over canonical(section_digests)
design.canonical: recursive JSON, object keys sorted, compact separators, JS-native number formatting (String(Number))
design.agreement_guarantee: build-time digests produced by executing the SAME canonical+SHA-256 JS that is embedded in the page (run in Node) -> browser recompute agrees byte-for-byte by construction; no Python/JS float-format divergence possible

in_browser_verifier.recompute: pure-JS SHA-256 + canonical serialiser embedded in ui_app.html
in_browser_verifier.renders: verified/altered per-section table + Overall INTEGRITY VERIFIED / CONTENT ALTERED badge in the Integrity (H1) panel

verification.build_time_node_crosscheck: embedded payload recomputes to identical section_digests + root_digest
verification.jsdom_in_browser_recompute: all 23 sections verified (22 ok rows shown + version section), badge INTEGRITY VERIFIED
verification.tamper_test: mutating the 'summary' section flips badge to CONTENT ALTERED with 1 altered row
verification.offline_self_tests: 8/8 ok:true (ui_app 358, offline_viewer 11, combined_gui 27, userrun 9, distribution 9, integrity 10, search_deeplink 18, bundle_printall 21)

constraints: NO model parameter changes; Phase 30 binding stop-rule honoured; MR-016/MR-017 owner decision not pre-empted; governed frozen-t headline 39975.654628199336 untouched
